title: 10 best herbal creams for hot flashes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with hot flashes because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The cream's active ingredients, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work together to calm the nervous system and regulate body temperature. This helps to alleviate the intense heat and sweating associated with hot flashes, providing relief from discomfort and anxiety. Additionally, valerian creams can also help to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by hot flashes, further reducing symptoms and improving overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian cream helps to reduce anxiety and stress associated with hot flashes, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Pain relief: Valerian contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate hot flash-related discomfort, such as breast tenderness, headaches, and joint pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Valerian's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve circulation, reducing the frequency and intensity of hot flashes by regulating body temperature and preventing overheating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used due to their high concentration of valepotriates, which have a sedative effect and help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the valerian plant contain volatile oils that can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making them an effective ingredient in hot flash creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots dig deeply around the edges of mature plants carefully.
Dry the harvested roots spread them thinly on paper towels for 7-10 days allowing air circulation.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle processing approximately 1 cup root material into 1/2 cup powdered valerian root.

Mix the ground valerian root with sweet almond oil beeswax cetyl alcohol and vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant bowl combining 10 grams valerian powder with 50 grams sweet almond oil 20 grams beeswax 5 grams cetyl alcohol and 2 grams vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture using a double boiler or a thermometer-controlled heating device until the beeswax melts preparing the cream within 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with hot flashes because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the body's heat. Herbal angelica extract contains a unique combination of compounds that have been shown to effectively reduce vasodilation, which is the primary cause of hot flashes. By constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation, angelica creams can help alleviate the uncomfortable symptoms associated with hot flashes, such as sweating, flushing, and palpitations. This natural approach can provide relief from these distressing episodes and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic Properties: Herbal angelica cream contains phytoestrogens, which can help alleviate hot flashes by binding to estrogen receptors, regulating hormonal imbalances, and reducing vasomotor symptoms.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, may reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash-related discomforts like sweating, flushing, and itching.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in angelica cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, Angelica roots help alleviate hot flash symptoms by soothing and calming the skin.
Rhyzomes: With its natural anti-inflammatory properties, Angelica rhizomes reduce redness and itching associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves, ensuring cleanliness and quality.
Chop the harvested herbs into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried angelica archangelica with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of aloe vera gel to the mixture, stirring well before pouring into sterilized containers.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature, then store it in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a unique blend of botanicals that work together to soothe and calm the body's natural response to hormonal fluctuations. The herb Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, has been traditionally used to ease anxiety and stress-related symptoms, which are often exacerbated by hot flashes. Additionally, the cream's cooling properties help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with hot flash episodes, providing relief from the distressing symptoms of menopause.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to reduce hot flash frequency and severity by calming the nervous system and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to hot flashes.
Valerian root: Has a sedative effect that helps to regulate body temperature and promote relaxation, reducing the discomfort associated with hot flashes.

Dong quai: Is used in traditional Chinese medicine to balance the body's energy (qi) and alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness by promoting hormonal balance and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to cool and calm the body's heat response.
Barks: Containing flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation caused by hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower herb passiflora incarnata at the recommended dosage of 20 grams.
Mix the dried passionflower herb with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture using a low heat setting on a double boiler to 60 degrees celsius over 10 minutes.
Strain the warm herbal extract from the oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container within 15 minutes.
Whisk together 100 milliliters of cooled cream base and 10 grams of vitamin e oil with the strained herbal extract in a stainless steel bowl for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with hot flashes because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has a direct impact on the body's thermoregulation. By stimulating the parasympathetic nervous system, hyperforin helps to reduce the body's production of heat-producing neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine. This leads to a decrease in hot flash frequency and severity, providing relief from the uncomfortable symptoms associated with menopause. Additionally, St John's Wort creams have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe skin irritation caused by hot flashes.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing sweating and discomfort.

Hypericin: As an antioxidant, hypericin helps reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hot flashes, and may also help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes.

Flavonoids and Phenolic Acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing symptoms of hot flashes such as anxiety, sweating, and rapid heartbeat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce vasodilation and alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe hot flash discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality st john's wort flowers and leaves dried to prevent oxidation and contamination.
Combine one part of the dried plant material with three parts of a carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before transferring it to airtight containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with hot flashes because they provide a soothing, cooling sensation that alleviates the intense heat and discomfort associated with this menopausal symptom. The calming properties of oats also help to reduce inflammation and relax the body, leading to a sense of calmness and reduced anxiety. Additionally, the natural ingredients in herbal oat creams can help regulate body temperature, providing long-lasting relief from hot flashes. This natural approach can be particularly effective for women seeking an alternative or complementary solution to traditional pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Oats contain a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation, providing relief from hot flash-related sweating and discomfort.

Antioxidant Properties: Oat extracts are rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms, promoting overall skin health and well-being.

Moisturizing Properties: The emollients present in oat creams help to lock in moisture and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by hot flashes and providing a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the oats plant are rich in saponins, which have been shown to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing hot flash symptoms.
Seeds: Oat seeds contain a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which has been found to reduce the severity and frequency of hot flashes by regulating hormonal changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried oat straw avena sativa flowers and leaves.
Combine the harvested botanicals with 250ml of organic jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture at 65-70°c for 2 hours and strain through cheesecloth to extract oils.
Mix 10g of beeswax pellets with 30g of shea butter and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Add the infused jojoba oil to the beeswax mixture and stir until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime creams helps with hot flashes because they contain natural compounds that have a cooling effect on the body. The herb's ability to soothe and calm the nervous system can help alleviate feelings of heat and anxiety associated with hot flashes. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce the discomfort and sweating caused by these episodes. As a result, herbal broad-leaved lime creams offer a natural and effective way to manage hot flashes, promoting a sense of calm and relief.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Tilia extracts contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash symptoms by neutralizing free radicals.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The essential oils present in Tilia flowers have been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, which may help calm the body and mind, reducing feelings of anxiety and discomfort associated with hot flashes.

Hormone-balancing activity: Some studies suggest that Tilia extracts may have a mild effect on hormone regulation, potentially helping to balance estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms related to menopause, including hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, roots have been traditionally used to reduce sweating and relieve symptoms of hot flashes.
Barks: The bark's tannins and phenolic compounds help to cool the body and alleviate hot flash-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia platyphyllos and dry them in shade at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 20g of dried tilia platyphyllos leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100ml of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture gently over low flame for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to separate the herbal extract from the oil and discard solids.
Store the tilia platyphyllos cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with hot flashes because they contain natural compounds that have a cooling effect on the body. The cream's active ingredients, such as citral and geraniol, work to reduce the production of sweat and relieve heat sensations. Additionally, lemon balm has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms like anxiety and restlessness often associated with hot flashes. By applying the cream to the skin, women can experience immediate relief from hot flash symptoms and enjoy a sense of calm and comfort throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with hot flashes.
GABA-ergic activity: The GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) present in lemon balm cream helps to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate anxiety and restlessness caused by hot flashes.
Hormone-balancing effects: Lemon balm's estrogen-like properties help to balance hormone levels, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes experienced during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of essential oils, which provide a calming and soothing effect on the skin, helping to reduce hot flash symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm have a gentle, cooling property that helps to calm the skin and relieve discomfort associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves by selecting fresh healthy specimens with no signs of disease or pests.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Combine 100g dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500ml carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks at room temperature stirring daily allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids before whipping with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hot flashes because they contain glycyrhizin, a natural compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the body. This compound helps to reduce inflammation and stress, which are common triggers for hot flashes. Additionally, licorice creams can help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to hot flash symptoms. By using an herbal licorice cream, women can experience relief from hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms, such as night sweats and mood swings.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce hot flash frequency and severity by inhibiting the production of luteinizing hormone, which regulates estrogen levels and can contribute to hot flashes.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body that can exacerbate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
Saponins: Have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to regulate body temperature and reduce sweating associated with hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes, due to their ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 10 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil and 5 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Pour the hot cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a unique blend of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins that work together to soothe and calm the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of stinging nettle help to reduce heat and discomfort associated with hot flashes, while its vasodilatory effects promote blood flow and relaxation. Additionally, the cream's ability to stabilize hormone levels can also contribute to a reduction in hot flash frequency and severity, providing much-needed relief for women experiencing menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and lignans, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash symptoms such as redness, swelling, and discomfort.

Estrogen-mimicking properties: Nettle contains estrogen-like compounds called isoflavones, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes, providing relief from sudden temperature changes and other symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in stinging nettle creams, such as histamine, may help dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and promoting a sense of coolness and relaxation, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms like sweating and flushing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate symptoms of hot flashes and reduce inflammation associated with hormonal fluctuations.
Rhyzomes: Contains antioxidants and flavonoids that may help regulate hormonal balance and soothe skin irritation caused by hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh stinging nettle leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the nettle leaves in a dehydrator at 35c for 24 hours or air-dry them for several days.
Combine 100 grams of dried nettle leaves with 200 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 70c.
Remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping into a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a unique combination of herbal extracts that work together to calm the body's nervous system and reduce inflammation. The lemon verbena itself has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe the skin and ease symptoms associated with hot flashes. Additionally, the cream's cooling sensation provides instant relief from heat and discomfort, making it an effective way to manage hot flash episodes and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemon verbena cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress caused by hot flashes, which can lead to cellular damage and inflammation.
Aromatic Relaxation Response: The calming aroma of lemon verbena in the cream stimulates a relaxation response in the body, helping to reduce anxiety and tension associated with hot flashes.

Vasodilatory Effects: Lemon verbena's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes by allowing heat to dissipate more efficiently from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm hot flash symptoms.
Buds: Contain volatile oils that provide a cooling and calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce hot flash discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems, then dry them thoroughly for at least two hours.
Combine one tablespoon of dried verbena officinalis with one cup of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herb's properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained oil with one tablespoon of beeswax and two tablespoons of coconut cream in a small double boiler, heating until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with hot flashes because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system, reducing the body's response to stress. The herb baicalin, found in skullcap, has a sedative effect that can help regulate bodily functions and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats. By applying topical creams containing skullcap extract, women can experience a decrease in the frequency and intensity of these uncomfortable symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relief throughout their day.
remedy_properties: Scutellarein: A flavonoid compound found in skullcap that has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing vasodilation and sweating.

Baicalin: A flavonoid glycoside in skullcap that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hot flashes, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Acacetin: A flavone compound found in skullcap that has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for managing hot flash symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create creams that help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids, rhyzomes contribute to the development of creams that soothe and calm hot flashes, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried scutellaria baicalensis roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered scutellaria baicalensis with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil to the scutellaria baicalensis and oil mixture and stir until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping with a hand mixer until smooth and creamy.
Pour the scutellaria baicalensis cream into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with hot flashes because they contain natural compounds that promote relaxation and calmness, thereby reducing symptoms of menopause. The cream's active ingredients, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the body, which helps to soothe and cool the skin. As a result, the cream provides instant relief from hot flashes by lowering body temperature and alleviating sweating, discomfort, and anxiety associated with menopause.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hot flashes, providing relief from symptoms such as redness, swelling, and irritation.

Calming effects on the nervous system: The soothing scent of lavender has been shown to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate hot flashes, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Some studies suggest that lavender may help regulate estrogen levels, which can contribute to hot flash relief by normalizing hormonal fluctuations and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain compounds like linalyl acetate, which help to reduce the severity of hot flashes by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.
Seeds: English lavender seeds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms associated with hot flashes, such as skin irritation and sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100 grams of organic coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c to 80°c for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets and stir until fully incorporated, allowing the mixture to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 60 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of sweet almond oil with the cooled cream base and blend thoroughly until well combined, taking an additional 5 minutes to prepare.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, labeling each jar with the preparation date and contents, and storing them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hot flashes because of its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate this symptom. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile also help to alleviate redness and discomfort associated with hot flashes. Additionally, chamomile's natural cooling effect can provide immediate relief from heat and sweating, helping to regulate body temperature and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to manage this common symptom of menopause.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile creams have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with hot flashes, providing relief from skin irritation.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile in creams can help ease muscle spasms and cramping, which often accompany hot flashes, reducing their severity and duration.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate hot flashes, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in relieving hot flash symptoms.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and other bioactive compounds, they have been traditionally used to calm nervousness and reduce sweating associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered chamomile with 20% beeswax and 80% sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 160-170°f for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture frequently until the wax is fully incorporated and the temperature reaches 110-120°f within 10-15 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool to room temperature with minimal stirring over 60-90 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer or whisk for 2-3 minutes then transfer it to sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often at the root of hot flashes. The cream's active ingredients, including Chinese angelica and red ginseng, work together to cool the body from within, reducing feelings of heat and sweating. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from hot flash-related discomforts such as dryness and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Reishi mushroom extract: Helps to reduce hot flashes by soothing the body's "fire" or stress response, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
Dong quai (Angelica sinensis): Relieves hot flashes by balancing hormonal fluctuations, particularly estrogen levels, which can help stabilize mood and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.
Licorice root: Soothes the body's "fire" or inflammation, reducing the intensity of hot flashes and night sweats while also calming digestive discomfort common in menopausal women.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm hot flashes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide relief from hot flash symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried ziziphus jujuba fruit powder and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove impurities.
Combine the sifted powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Add 50 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully melted, then remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 10 minutes.
Transfer the cream to sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator at 40°f to 45°f for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with hot flashes because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as damascena oil and beta-sitosterol, work together to calm the body's response to hormonal fluctuations, reducing the severity and frequency of hot flashes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing and nourishing qualities help to soothe skin irritation and discomfort often associated with hot flashes, providing a sense of relief and comfort to those experiencing them.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Damask rose contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to hot flashes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Estrogen-balancing properties: The estrogen-like compounds present in damask rose, such as phytoestrogens, can help regulate hormonal imbalances that cause hot flashes by providing a gentle and natural boost to estrogen levels.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties: Damask rose contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and muscle spasms associated with hot flashes, providing relief from discomfort and flushing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm hot flash symptoms.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce heat and discomfort associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosa damascena flowers weighing approximately 20 grams for infusion.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a strong floral extract.
Combine the cooled floral extract with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax pellets.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat stirring frequently until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set before storing in a dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a potent extract of Echinacea purpurea, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to alleviate menopausal symptoms. The cream works by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, which can help to alleviate the discomfort and heat associated with hot flashes. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of purple coneflower may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes, providing relief from these unpredictable and uncomfortable episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Herbal purple coneflower creams may help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation, which can contribute to heat intolerance and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in purple coneflower cream may help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hot flashes, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall well-being.

Estrogen-mimicking activity: Some herbal extracts in purple coneflower creams, such as isoflavones, may mimic the effects of estrogen to help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with menopause, including hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of purple coneflower contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to help reduce symptoms of hot flashes.
Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which can help to alleviate the inflammatory response associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the oil to extract the bioactive compounds from the herbs.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Whip the reserved liquid with 100 milliliters of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter until smooth and creamy, preparing the final product for packaging and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a combination of natural ingredients that help to calm and soothe the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of dandelion root and leaf work together to reduce heat and alleviate symptoms such as sweating, flushing, and chills. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate and cool the skin, providing relief from hot flash episodes. By addressing these physical manifestations of menopause, herbal dandelion creams can offer a natural and effective solution for managing hot flashes.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion cream's diuretic properties can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing fluid retention, which can contribute to feelings of warmth and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion cream may help soothe and calm the body's response to hot flashes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with this symptom.

Hormone-balancing properties: Dandelion root has been traditionally used to support hormone balance and regulate menstrual cycles, which can help alleviate hot flashes by addressing underlying hormonal imbalances that may be contributing to these symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, dandelion roots help reduce hot flash symptoms by soothing and calming the body.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits contain valuable antioxidants and phytoestrogens, which help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate hot flash discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh taraxacum officinale leaves measuring 100g for infusion.
Combine gathered leaves with 500ml of boiling distilled water and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth and discard solids after 10 minutes.
Mix the resulting liquid with 200g of cold-pressed organic coconut oil and 20g of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for 45 minutes until melted and blended thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hot flashes because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that soothes the skin and calms the body. Curcumin's ability to reduce inflammation and modulate hormonal imbalances makes it an effective remedy for alleviating hot flash symptoms such as sweating, flushing, and rapid heartbeat. By applying turmeric creams directly to the skin, women can experience quick relief from hot flashes and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Vasodilatory effects: Turmeric creams containing vasodilators like curcumin can help dilate blood vessels, which can lead to improved circulation and reduced heat flux associated with hot flashes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams, such as vitamin C and E, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the turmeric plant are rich in essential oils, including turmerone, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease hot flash discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 tablespoons of dried turmeric roots and mix with 1 tablespoon of sweet almond oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens and reaches 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to 120°f, then add 1 teaspoon of beeswax pellets and stir until melted.
Gradually add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and let set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hot flashes are a natural remedy that combines the soothing benefits of herbs with moisturizing properties to alleviate symptoms of hot flashes. These creams work by calming the body's temperature regulation, reducing sweating and discomfort, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal creams effective in managing hot flashes include those infused with dong quai, black cohosh, and chasteberry. By using these creams, women experiencing frequent hot flashes can enjoy improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety, and increased confidence, ultimately enhancing their overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hot flashes with herbal creams is by combining cooling botanicals like peppermint, chamomile, and lavender with moisturizing ingredients like coconut oil or shea butter. Look for products containing damiana, a natural sedative that helps regulate body temperature. Apply the cream regularly throughout the day, especially before bedtime, to help reduce hot flash frequency and severity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hot flashes that are treated with herbal creams are menopausal symptoms, night sweats, and skin irritations such as eczema and acne. Herbal creams containing ingredients like dong quai, black cohosh, and evening primrose oil can help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, cooling the skin, and regulating hormones. These natural remedies offer a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing hot flash-like conditions, promoting overall wellness and comfort.
